How to Run ETLDelphi

ETLDelphi

2026-03-03

Source: vignettes/how_to_run.Rmd
how_to_run.Rmd

Overview

The recommended ETLDelphi workflow has three steps:

  1. Create a fresh DuckDB database and load the OMOP vocabulary with init_vocabulary_db().
  2. Load the Delphi CSV files into the src schema with init_source_data().
  3. Run the core SQL ETL with run_etl().

By default, the package uses these schemas:

  • src for raw Delphi source tables
  • stg for typed staging, mapping, reject, and QC tables
  • main for OMOP CDM and vocabulary tables

Those defaults come from default_etl_config(). You only need to override them when your database layout is different.

What You Need

Before running the ETL, you need:

  • a directory containing OMOP vocabulary files such as CONCEPT.csv, CONCEPT_RELATIONSHIP.csv, and VOCABULARY.csv
  • a directory containing the Delphi source CSV files such as enrollment.csv, encounter.csv, lab_results.csv, and medication_orders.csv
  • the DBI, duckdb, and ETLDelphi packages installed

The package creates the CDM tables and vocabulary tables for you. You do not need to pre-create the OMOP schema manually when you use init_vocabulary_db().

End-to-End Run

This is the shortest reliable way to run the ETL from scratch.

library(DBI)
library(duckdb)
library(ETLDelphi)

db_path <- "path/to/delphi.duckdb"
vocabulary_dir <- "path/to/vocabulary_download_v5"
source_dir <- "path/to/delphi_csvs"

con <- init_vocabulary_db(
  db_path = db_path,
  vocabulary_dir = vocabulary_dir,
  cdm_schema = "main"
)

init_source_data(con, source_dir)

cfg <- default_etl_config()
run_etl(con, config = cfg)

DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

What this does:

  • creates the DuckDB file if it does not exist
  • creates the OMOP CDM 5.4 tables in main
  • loads the vocabulary into main
  • recreates src and loads the Delphi CSVs
  • runs the ordered SQL pipeline under inst/sql

Common Run Modes

Dry Run

Use this to see which SQL files would execute without changing the database.

con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), db_path)
run_etl(con, dry_run = TRUE)
DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

Re-run From a Later Step

If the source and staging data are already loaded, you can restart from a later phase.

con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), db_path)

# Rebuild vocabulary-driven maps and everything after them
run_etl(con, from_step = "30_vocab")

# Run only the QC phase
run_etl(con, from_step = "90_qc")

DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

Stop At A Specific Step

This is useful when debugging a specific phase.

con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), db_path)

# Stop after visit_occurrence is loaded
run_etl(con, to_step = "54_load_visit_occurrence")

DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

Configuration

run_etl() uses default_etl_config() when you do not pass a config. That object includes:

  • schema names
  • the sentinel concept ID for unmapped concepts (0L)
  • accepted date formats for staging
  • standard OMOP type concept IDs used by the loaders
  • optional paths for custom mapping CSVs

To override a setting, start from the defaults and edit only what you need.

cfg <- default_etl_config()

# Example: use a different CDM schema
cfg$schemas$cdm <- "omop"

# Example: point to project-specific mapping overrides
cfg$custom_mapping_path <- "inst/extdata/custom_concept_mapping.csv"
cfg$custom_ndc_mapping_path <- "inst/extdata/custom_ndc_mapping.csv"

con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), db_path)
run_etl(con, config = cfg)
DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

If you change cfg$schemas$cdm, pass the same schema name to init_vocabulary_db(cdm_schema = ...) when you create the database.

What To Check After The Run

The ETL writes useful diagnostics into stg:

  • stg.etl_run_log and stg.etl_step_log show which SQL files ran
  • stg.qc_row_counts summarizes table populations
  • stg.qc_mapping_coverage shows how many rows still use concept ID 0
  • stg.qc_parse_failures summarizes reject-table counts
  • stg.qc_required_fields highlights required-field violations
  • stg.domain_routing_log records rows that were moved into a different OMOP domain

Example queries:

con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), db_path)

DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT * FROM stg.qc_row_counts ORDER BY table_name")
DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT * FROM stg.qc_mapping_coverage ORDER BY cdm_table")
DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT * FROM stg.qc_parse_failures ORDER BY reject_table")

DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

Optional: Genomic Extension

The genomic simulation and genomic CDM extension are separate from the core ETL. Run them only after the core ETL has completed successfully.

library(CDMConnector)

con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), db_path)

cdm <- cdmFromCon(con, cdmSchema = "main", writeSchema = "main")
cdm <- simulateGenomicData(cdm, overwrite = TRUE)

run_genomic_etl(con, config = default_etl_config())

cdmDisconnect(cdm)
DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

Summary

For most users, the only functions you need are:

  • init_vocabulary_db()
  • init_source_data()
  • run_etl()

Start with default_etl_config(), override only the settings you need, and check the stg.qc_* tables after every run.
